name: Jerusalem
desc: Capital of Israel and Judah; site of the Temple, the Crucifixion, Resurrection, and Pentecost.

name: Bethlehem
desc: Birthplace of David and of Jesus, fulfilling Micah's prophecy.

name: Nazareth
desc: Where Jesus grew up and spent thirty years before his public ministry.

name: Capernaum
desc: Jesus's base of ministry in Galilee; home of Peter; site of the Bread of Life discourse.

name: Jericho
desc: The oldest city in the world; its walls fell at Israel's shout under Joshua.

name: Hebron
desc: Abraham's home in Canaan; site of the cave of Machpelah where the patriarchs are buried.

name: Beersheba
desc: Southernmost city of the promised land — 'from Dan to Beersheba' marked all Israel.

name: Gaza
desc: Philistine stronghold where Samson was imprisoned and died pulling down Dagon's temple.

name: Samaria
desc: Capital of the northern kingdom of Israel, built by Omri; fell to Assyria in 722 BC.

name: Shechem
desc: Abraham's first stop in Canaan; site of Jacob's well where Jesus spoke with the Samaritan woman.

name: Bethel
desc: Where Jacob saw the ladder reaching to heaven and named it 'house of God'.

name: Shiloh
desc: The Tabernacle rested here for ~300 years; where Joshua divided the land among the tribes.

name: Megiddo
desc: Strategic fortress city guarding the Jezreel Valley pass; site of many decisive battles.

name: Caesarea
desc: Herod's great port city; where Peter preached to Cornelius and Paul was imprisoned for two years.

name: Joppa
desc: Ancient port city; Jonah boarded a ship here to flee from God; Peter raised Dorcas here.

name: Tyre
desc: Premier Phoenician port city; Hiram supplied cedar and craftsmen for Solomon's Temple.

name: Sidon
desc: Leading Phoenician city; Jezebel was a Sidonian princess; Elijah was sustained near here.

name: Dan
desc: Northernmost city of Israel; Jeroboam erected a golden calf here; 'from Dan to Beersheba'.

name: Lachish
desc: Second-most important city of Judah; famously besieged by Sennacherib, depicted in Nineveh palace reliefs.

name: Hazor
desc: 'Formerly the head of all those kingdoms'; Joshua burned it; archaeology confirms the conquest destruction layer.

name: Gibeon
desc: Where the sun stood still a full day as Joshua defeated the five-king coalition.

name: Gilgal
desc: Israel's base camp after crossing the Jordan; where the reproach of Egypt was rolled away.

name: Ai
desc: Site of Israel's initial defeat (due to Achan's sin), then taken by a masterful ambush under Joshua.

name: Dothan
desc: Where Joseph was sold by his brothers to an Ishmaelite caravan; also Elisha saw the chariots of fire.

name: Jezreel
desc: Ahab's palace; site of Naboth's vineyard; scene of Jehu's execution of the Omri dynasty.

name: Caesarea Philippi
desc: At the foot of Mount Hermon where Peter confessed: 'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.'

name: Emmaus
desc: Two disciples met the risen Jesus on this road; he revealed himself in the breaking of bread.

name: Bethany
desc: Home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus; Jesus raised Lazarus here four days after his death.

name: Qumran
desc: Community on the Dead Sea shore where the Dead Sea Scrolls were hidden in caves.

name: Ashdod
desc: Philistine city where the captured ark was placed in Dagon's temple; Dagon fell face-down before it.

name: Gath
desc: Hometown of Goliath; David feigned madness here before King Achish while fleeing Saul.

name: Aphek
desc: Where the Philistines captured the ark of God; 30,000 Israelites fell; Hophni and Phinehas died.

name: Tirzah
desc: First capital of the northern kingdom before Samaria was built; praised for beauty in Song of Solomon.

name: Mahanaim
desc: Where angels met Jacob returning from Haran; later where David fled during Absalom's revolt.

name: Peniel
desc: Where Jacob wrestled with God until dawn and received the name Israel — 'one who strives with God'.

name: Ziklag
desc: Given to David by Achish; the Amalekites burned it; David heard of Saul's death here.

name: Rameses
desc: The city built by Israelite forced labor in Egypt; Israel set out from here on Passover night.

name: Sinai
desc: The mountain of God; where the Ten Commandments were given, the covenant ratified, and the Tabernacle built.

name: Kadesh-barnea
desc: Where the twelve spies returned; Israel's unbelief condemned them to forty years of wilderness wandering.

name: Ezion-geber
desc: Port at the tip of the Gulf of Aqaba; Solomon built his fleet here for the Ophir trade voyages.

name: Nebo
desc: Where Moses saw the promised land and died at 120 years old; God himself buried him here.

name: Babylon
desc: Capital of Nebuchadnezzar's empire; the exiles were brought here; Daniel served in its court.

name: Nineveh
desc: Capital of Assyria; Jonah preached here and they repented; Nahum prophesied its destruction in 612 BC.

name: Ur
desc: Abraham's homeland in southern Mesopotamia; God called him out to go to an unknown land on faith alone.

name: Haran
desc: Abraham's family stopped here; Jacob fled here from Esau and served Laban twenty years.

name: Carchemish
desc: Where Nebuchadnezzar crushed Egypt in 605 BC — the decisive battle that began the Babylonian era.

name: Susa
desc: Persian winter capital; where Esther saved the Jewish people and Nehemiah served as cupbearer to Artaxerxes.

name: Persepolis
desc: Ceremonial capital of the Persian empire; built by Darius and Xerxes who authorized the return from exile.

name: Nippur
desc: Major Babylonian city where many Jewish exiles settled; business tablets preserve dozens of Hebrew names.

name: Damascus
desc: Capital of Aram; David conquered it; fell to Tiglath-Pileser; Paul was converted on the road here.

name: Memphis
desc: Ancient capital of Egypt during the Patriarchal period and Exodus; Isaiah prophesied its desolation.

name: Antioch
desc: The first great Gentile church; where believers were first called 'Christians'; sending base for Paul's journeys.

name: Ephesus
desc: Largest port in Asia Minor; Paul spent three years here; home of one of the seven churches of Revelation.

name: Corinth
desc: Cosmopolitan port city; Paul spent 18 months here and wrote Romans from this city.

name: Athens
desc: Paul preached on the Areopagus, declaring the identity of the Unknown God and proclaiming the resurrection.

name: Thessalonica
desc: Paul planted a church here in three Sabbaths; he wrote 1–2 Thessalonians to encourage this persecuted congregation.

name: Philippi
desc: The first church planted in Europe; Lydia was its first convert; Paul wrote his most joyful letter to it.

name: Rome
desc: Paul arrived under house arrest and spent two years preaching the kingdom to all who came.

name: Troas
desc: Paul received the Macedonian vision here — 'Come over to Macedonia and help us' — and the gospel entered Europe.

name: Malta
desc: Paul was shipwrecked here; he survived a viper bite and healed many on the island over three months.

name: Lystra
desc: Paul healed a lame man here, was stoned and left for dead; Timothy — his closest companion — was from Lystra.

name: Patmos
desc: The island where John was exiled and received the entire book of Revelation from the risen Christ.

name: Smyrna
desc: A persecuted church receiving no rebuke from Christ; Polycarp was martyred here c. AD 155.

name: Pergamum
desc: 'Where Satan's throne is'; the church held fast even where Antipas was martyred for his faith.

name: Sardis
desc: 'A reputation for being alive, but you are dead' — a church as complacent as its twice-captured citadel.

name: Laodicea
desc: The self-sufficient, lukewarm church; Christ stands outside knocking: 'If anyone hears my voice and opens the door…'

name: Philadelphia
desc: Faithful under weakness; Christ set before them an open door no one can shut.

name: Thyatira
desc: A trade-guild city where Lydia was from; the church tolerated a false prophetess teaching compromise.

name: Rabbah
desc: Capital of Ammon (modern Amman); besieged by Joab during David's sin with Bathsheba.

name: Marah
desc: The first test after the Red Sea — bitter water made sweet when Moses cast a tree into it.

name: Riblah
desc: Nebuchadnezzar's field headquarters; where Zedekiah's sons were killed before his eyes before blinding.

name: Zoar
desc: The small city Lot fled to when Sodom was destroyed by fire and sulfur from heaven.

name: Moriah
desc: Where Abraham bound Isaac in obedience to God; the same mountain where Solomon built the Temple.

name: Valley of Elah
desc: Where David killed Goliath with a stone from the brook — the defining confrontation of the young shepherd's faith.

name: Mizpah
desc: Where Samuel gathered Israel to repent; later where the tribes mustered after the Gibeah atrocity.

name: Galilee
desc: Northern region of Palestine around the Sea of Galilee; home of Nazareth and Capernaum, and the base of most of Jesus's public ministry.

name: Judea
desc: Southern region of Palestine centered on Jerusalem; the heartland of the old kingdom of Judah and the stage of the Passion week.

name: Sea of Galilee
desc: Freshwater lake in northern Palestine; scene of the calling of the fishermen, the stilling of the storm, and Jesus walking on water.

name: Decapolis
desc: League of ten Hellenistic cities east and south of the Sea of Galilee; largely Gentile territory where the healed demoniac proclaimed Jesus.

name: Perea
desc: Region east of the Jordan ruled by Herod Antipas; John baptized here, and Jesus ministered here on the way to His final Passover.

name: Phoenicia
desc: Coastal region north of Galilee with the great ports of Tyre and Sidon; Jesus healed the Syrophoenician woman's daughter here.

name: Canaan
desc: The land promised to Abraham and his offspring; conquered under Joshua and apportioned among the twelve tribes.

name: Egypt
desc: Great kingdom of the Nile; Israel's house of bondage, refuge of the patriarchs and of the child Jesus, and the land of the Exodus.

name: Assyria
desc: Mesopotamian empire centered on Nineveh; instrument of judgment that carried the northern kingdom into exile in 722 BC.

name: Babylonia
desc: Empire of Nebuchadnezzar centered on Babylon; destroyed Jerusalem in 586 BC and held Judah in seventy years of exile.

name: Philistia
desc: Coastal plain of the five Philistine cities — Gaza, Ashdod, Ashkelon, Gath, Ekron; Israel's chief rival from the judges to David.

name: Edom
desc: Mountain kingdom of Esau's descendants south of the Dead Sea; judged by the prophets for its violence against brother Jacob.

name: Moab
desc: Kingdom east of the Dead Sea; home of Ruth, refuge of David's parents, and the plateau where Moses viewed the promised land.

name: Ammon
desc: Kingdom east of the Jordan around Rabbah (modern Amman); recurring adversary of Israel from the judges to the exile.

name: Mesopotamia
desc: The land between the Tigris and Euphrates — cradle of Ur and Haran, homeland of Abraham before God's call.

name: Persia
desc: Empire of Cyrus, Darius, and Artaxerxes that succeeded Babylon; its kings decreed the return from exile and the rebuilding of Jerusalem.

name: Macedonia
desc: Roman province of northern Greece; Paul crossed here after the Macedonian vision, planting Philippi, Thessalonica, and Berea.

name: Achaia
desc: Roman province of southern Greece with Athens and Corinth; Paul ministered eighteen months in Corinth under Gallio's rule.

name: Cyprus
desc: Mediterranean island and Barnabas's homeland; first stop of the first missionary journey, where the proconsul Sergius Paulus believed.

name: Crete
desc: Large Mediterranean island passed on Paul's voyage to Rome; Titus was later left here to set the churches in order.
